CheckTitle: AI agent assumed-role sessions must not exceed 1 hour
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Permissions**. Checks that AI agent assumed-role sessions are configured with a maximum duration of 3600 seconds (1 hour) or less. Short-lived credentials limit the window of opportunity for a stolen token to be exploited. A session with no explicit limit or a duration longer than one hour represents an unnecessarily extended exposure window.
Risk: Long-lived session tokens obtained by an attacker through prompt injection, SSRF, or metadata endpoint access remain valid for the full session duration. A 12-hour session token stolen at the start of a workday gives an attacker an extended operational window with no need to re-authenticate or rotate the credential.

Code.Other:
1. Update the maximum session duration on the role: `aws iam update-role --role-name <role> --max-session-duration 3600`.
2. If the agent uses `sts:AssumeRole` directly, ensure the `DurationSeconds` parameter is set to 3600 or less in the assume-role call.
3. Verify the change: `aws iam get-role --role-name <role> --query Role.MaxSessionDuration`.
4. Review all code paths that call `sts:AssumeRole` to confirm they specify an explicit duration.
5. Update the agent manifest `session_duration_seconds` field to reflect the new maximum.
6. Monitor CloudTrail for `AssumeRole` calls requesting durations beyond 3600 seconds.

Recommendation.Text: Set the IAM role `MaxSessionDuration` to 3600 seconds for all AI agent roles. Design agents to refresh their credentials before expiry rather than requesting long-lived tokens. Use instance profiles or ECS task roles where possible, as these automatically rotate credentials. Alert on any `AssumeRole` call requesting more than 3600 seconds.

Notes: ASPM-024 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
